commodities.name: Wheat
commodities.detail: CBOT SRW wheat at $7.42/bushel (April 2). Up 32% from pre-war $5.62. Fertilizer disruptions from Hormuz closure (+40% urea, +41% ammonia) driving 2026 crop cost increases. ~1/3 of global fertilizer transits Hormuz. UN humanitarian/fertilizer corridor agreed March 27 but barely operational. GCC food imports disrupted 70%. Brent at $106 adding to transport costs.

commodities.name: Corn
commodities.detail: CBOT May corn at $4.56/bushel (April 1). Up from pre-war $4.31. USDA Prospective Plantings showed corn acreage down 3.45M acres YoY as fertilizer costs shift acres to soybeans. USDA projects $4.20 marketing year average.

commodities.name: Soybeans
commodities.detail: CBOT soybeans (May) at $11.61/bushel. Up 14.7% from pre-war $10.12. Managed money at 4-year high net long in grains. Soybean oil surging on biofuel demand. Palm oil also elevated. One-third of globally traded fertilizer transits through Hormuz.
commodities.source: Brownfield Ag News / CME March 20 closing

commodities.name: Rice
commodities.detail: Asian import routes through Persian Gulf completely disrupted. Export restrictions emerging in Southeast Asia.

commodities.name: Sugar
commodities.detail: Sugar #11 (ICE) at $0.224/lb (April 2). Prices reversing earlier decline as shipping costs and diesel surcharges ripple through global supply chains. Hormuz closure driving up transport costs even for non-Gulf commodities. Sugar now modestly above pre-war levels after lagging other commodities.

commodities.name: Cooking Oil
commodities.detail: Palm oil: shipping insurance costs up 10x (0.25% to ~10% of vessel value per Lloyd's List), spiking transport costs. War risk insurance adds $3-5/MT to global commodity delivery.

commodities.name: Coffee
commodities.detail: Global shipping costs and fuel surcharges rising sharply. Container rates up 85% on key routes.

commodities.name: Beef
commodities.detail: Feed costs up due to grain price spikes. Diesel-dependent supply chain passing costs to consumers.

groceryImpact.note: USDA projects 3.1% food-at-home inflation in 2026. Wolfe Research estimates war could add ~2 additional percentage points. Fertilizer crisis (urea +40%, ammonia +41%) threatening Northern Hemisphere planting season.

fertilizer.detail: Approximately one-third of globally traded fertilizer transits the Strait of Hormuz. Urea surged from $475 to $665/tonne (+40%). Ammonia prices up 41% YoY. Worst timing: start of Northern Hemisphere planting season. 25% of US farmers have NOT purchased fertilizer for planting season. Wolfe Research estimates food-at-home inflation could rise ~2 percentage points. USDA predicts 3.1% food price rise in 2026.

context: War-driven supply disruptions continue to push food prices higher. Fertilizer crisis intensifying: urea +40% ($665/tonne), ammonia +41% YoY. ~1/3 of global fertilizer transits Hormuz. USDA predicts 3.1% food price rise in 2026. WFP: 45M more could face acute hunger. Diesel at $5.45/gal rippling through food transport. 25% of US farmers have NOT purchased fertilizer for planting season. USDA Prospective Plantings: corn acreage down 3.45M acres YoY. GCC food imports disrupted 70%. Gas $4.06/gal adds to transportation costs. Brent $106.42 after Trump's April 6 Hormuz deadline. IEA warns 'April worse than March' for supply.
